paragraphs.body: The Gates of Campus In My Mind – My Visit To University of Pittsburgh Main Point: This story talks about how I imagined the University of Pittsburgh before visiting it, what I discovered when I got there, and how it is different from a university in my hometown. I realized that different cultures think about campuses in different ways, and these differences tell us something about how people live and learn.

Exposition Place: The University of Pittsburgh campus. -What I Imagined: Before visiting, I thought the University of Pittsburgh would have a big gate or entrance. I imagined something like a stone arch or a large sign with the university’s name on it.

-I thought the campus would be in one area, surrounded by walls or fences, and everything— classrooms, dorms, and offices—would be inside. -This idea came from my experience with Chinese universities, where there’s usually a main gate, walls, and everything is built close together. What I Found: -When I arrived, I was surprised! The University of Pittsburgh doesn’t have a big gate or clear entrance. Instead, the campus is open and spread out across the city.
-What Surprised Me: The buildings are mixed with shops, streets, and other places. It’s hard to tell where the campus starts or ends.
-How I Felt: At first, I was confused because I couldn’t find the “main entrance.” It felt very different from what I expected.

Rising Action -Comparison to My Hometown University: Similarities: Both places have libraries, classrooms, and places for students to study and hang out. -Differences: In my hometown, the campus has clear walls and a big gate that shows where the university starts. At the University of Pittsburgh, it feels more like part of the city, with no clear borders or big entrance. -What This Shows: In my hometown, the design makes you feel like you’re in a special, separate place for learning. In Pittsburgh, the open design makes it feel connected to the city and people around it.

Climax -What I Learned from Comparing the Two: This made me think about how different cultures design campuses. Chinese campuses are more closed and focused, which makes students feel like they belong to a special group. Pittsburgh’s campus is more open, which shows a focus on freedom, sharing, and being part of the community. This comparison helped me understand how spaces reflect values. It also made me see that there are many ways to think about a “campus.”

Resolution -How This Changed My Thinking: Visiting the University of Pittsburgh taught me something new. I learned that campuses can be open and still feel like a place for learning. I also learned that in my hometown, having walls and gates can make people feel proud of being part of the university. This experience helped me understand how spaces can shape how people feel and think in different cultures.
